PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE IS GRANTED IN PART. CGC-22-598415 (SHULL) AND CGC-20-587976 (KING) ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR TRIAL ONLY. (SEE SANCHEZ V SUPERIOR COURT (1988) 203 CAL.APP.3D 1391.) THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS THE PARTIES TO COORDINATE DISCOVERY (E.G. DEPOSITIONS REGARDING LIABILITY) AND SUBSEQUENT HEARINGS (E.G. MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT) TO PRESERVE PARTY AND JUDICIAL RESOURCES AND TO AVOID INCONSISTENT RULINGS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/SRB)

Motion For Leave To File Complaint-In-Intervention; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Austin Smith
Off Calendar Jul-20-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. AUSTIN SMITH, Esq. of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP (Tel: 213-542-8000) for plaintiff-intervention Wilson Construction Company;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: INTERVENOR WILSON CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S Motion For Leave To File Complaint-In-Intervention.

The moving party informs the Court it filed a notice taking this motion off calendar. Based on this, the Court vacates its tentative ruling, and orders the matter off calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jamie Goldstein, Esq. (312.217.9087; jamie@aswtlawyers.com) of Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Torrijos LLP for Plaintiffs.
Jonathan Hembree, Esq. (626.765.3000; jhembree@cunninghamswaim.com) of Cunningham Swaim LLP for Defendant/moving party, P.J. Helicopters, Inc.
Jennifer Dodge, Esq. (310.372.3344; jdodgelaw@jenniferdodgelaw.com) for Defendant, Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT P.J. HELICOPTERS, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is taken under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Joanna Broadwell, CSR# 10959, (510.332.0512; broadwelljo@aol.com), Reported. =(302/RBU)

***Order sustaining with leave to amend, defendant P.J. Helicopters, Inc.'s demurrer to complaint is signed after hearing.***

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-587976 WITH CASE CGC-22-598415
Off Calendar May-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS THOMAS SHULL, DEBRA JUDD, NANCY JORDAN and GAYLORD BRIGGS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-587976 WITH CASE CGC-22-598415.

Plaintiff's motion to consolidate is granted in part.

CGC-22-598415 (Shull) and CGC-20-587976 (King) are consolidated for trial only. (See Sanchez v Superior Court (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1391.)

The court further orders the parties to coordinate discovery (e.g. depositions regarding liability) and subsequent hearings (e.g. motions for summary judgment) to preserve party and judicial resources and to avoid inconsistent rulings.

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)
